danger_signals.visual_form: Undead pirate character model with skeletal/demonic appearance; later manifests as fiery demon form
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist and existential threat; transformation indicates escalating danger level

danger_signals.visual_form: Diamond ring with curse property (gold/magical aura implied)
danger_signals.meaning: Interactable hazards that trigger negative consequences; curse transforms Elaine into gold statue

danger_signals.visual_form: Pirate enemy characters during sea combat sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounters; players must engage in ship-to-ship battles and insult sword fighting

danger_signals.visual_form: Storm weather event; volcano presence on Blood Island
danger_signals.meaning: Natural dangers that drive narrative progression and force location changes

safety_signals.visual_form: The Voodoo Lady character model; Wally the cartographer; crew members
safety_signals.meaning: Quest givers and allies; provide guidance and assistance in overcoming obstacles

safety_signals.visual_form: Chicken restaurant, barber shop, theater, exclusive beach club, hotel
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones where Guybrush conducts non-combat activities and social puzzle-solving

safety_signals.visual_form: Items added to inventory chest; visual confirmation of puzzle completion
safety_signals.meaning: Progress indicator; collecting items moves player toward goal of obtaining non-cursed ring

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Commandeered pirate ship; barber crew aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Plunder Island, Blood Island as territories Guybrush controls/operates within

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Undead pirate aesthetic; 'Carnival of the Damned' setting
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Skeletal pirate form transitions to fiery demon form in final confrontation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Captain Rottingham and rival pirate captains
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy ship designs; opposing combatants in sea battles

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cannibals from Monkey Island (now volcano-worshipping vegetarians)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Blood Island; volcano worship sites; native settlements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Elaine transformed into gold statue; immobilized form contrasts with normal character model
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guybrush unable to interact normally with Elaine; drives all subsequent quest objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Voodoo cannonball explosion effect; villain temporarily removed from scene
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guybrush freed to propose; false sense of safety before villain resurrection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fiery demon form replaces skeletal pirate; escalated visual threat level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: LeChuck pursues Guybrush with renewed intent; final confrontation trigger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental weather effects; ship crash landing on Blood Island
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced location change; narrative progression via natural disaster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Inventory chest fills with recovered items; visual confirmation of puzzle solutions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to new locations and NPCs unlocked as items collected

environmental_storytelling.example: Barber crew members identified by barber shop aesthetics; Stan the salesman character recurrence indicates commercial faction presence
Chicken restaurant, barber shop, theater, beach club each serve as microenvironments encoding social/commercial activities rather than combat
Progression from Plunder Island (initial safe haven) → sea routes → Blood Island (final challenge) marks escalating danger and quest advancement
Cannibals' transformation into volcano-worshipping vegetarians indicates cultural shift and native faction characterization
Diamond ring carries hidden curse; visual ambiguity about object safety teaches player that treasure can harbor danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Interacts with environment using coin menu (hand for physical actions, skull for observation, parrot for dialogue)
Engages in insult sword fighting (rhyming retorts) or ship-to-ship cannon combat with difficulty options
Quest-driven locomotion; pursues ring acquisition as primary objective
Pursuing antagonist; captures and menaces Elaine; engages Guybrush in confrontations
Escalated threat; bent on forcing Elaine into unwanted marriage; final boss encounter trigger
Immobilized; becomes object of quest rather than active participant
Provides exposition and guidance; establishes quest objectives; supernatural advisor role
Steals map; serves as intermediate boss requiring insult sword fighting victory
Assist in ship-to-ship combat if player delegates; otherwise player controls battles directly

notes: The Curse of Monkey Island employs cartoon cel-animation style to soften danger signals while maintaining clear visual hierarchy of threats. The coin-shaped action menu (hand/skull/parrot icons) creates visual language for interaction types—this is a manifestation system that trains player understanding of world interactivity. Cursed objects represent a key danger signal unique to this game's mechanics: treasure that harms rather than helps. The transformation of Elaine into a gold statue is a stark visual commitment device that motivates all subsequent behavior. LeChuck's form shift from undead pirate to fiery demon visually escalates threat level and world-state corruption. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on location typing (safe commercial/social zones vs. hostile sea/island zones) rather than environmental decay or visual corruption.
